merged_findings: Consistent strong linear dependencies with correlation near or equal to 1 are observed across features e13 to e22, indicating significant redundancy within this cluster.

Several feature pairs, including Average Packet Size and Packet Length Mean, as well as Fwd Header Length and its duplicate, show near-perfect linear correlation, supporting redundancy likely introduced by preprocessing or feature engineering rather than inherent protocol characteristics.

Cardinality verification on sampled features (e13, e14, e15) confirms low cardinality consistent with near-constant or highly dependent behavior, supporting effective dimensionality reduction within this feature subset.
Local consistency checks reveal moderate label conflicts that do not undermine the classification of these dependencies as redundant.
No evidence of instability or temporal variation in dependency metrics was found in the localized context.
open_gaps: Analysis is limited to local dependency clusters without cross-segment aggregation to verify if redundancy patterns hold globally.
No direct distribution verification was performed due to bounded step budget.
Cardinality verification was limited to a subset of features and not exhaustively applied to the full feature set.
update_focus: Focus on extending verification of redundancy patterns beyond local clusters to cross-segment and global contexts, and on comprehensive cardinality and distribution analyses to fully characterize feature dependencies.
